# servicenow_encryption_context (Resource)

`servicenow_encryption_context` manages an encryption context record within ServiceNow Edge Encryption. Requires the Edge Encryption plugin to be active on the target instance.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages an encryption context record in ServiceNow Edge Encryption.
#
# NOTE: Requires the Edge Encryption plugin to be installed and activated on
# the target ServiceNow instance. On instances without the plugin the
# sys_encryption_context table does not exist and this resource cannot be used.

resource "servicenow_encryption_context" "example" {
  name        = "example-encryption-context"
  type        = "standard"
  description = "Example encryption context managed by Terraform"
  algorithm   = "AES_256"
  active      = true

  # Sensitive: not read back from ServiceNow once set.
  encryption_key = "base64-encoded-key-material"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name of the encryption context record.

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) If set to 'true', the encryption context is active.
- `algorithm` (String) Encryption algorithm to use. Allowed values are AES_128 or AES_256.
- `description` (String) Description of the encryption context.
- `encryption_key` (String, Sensitive) Encryption key associated with the context. Sensitive: the value is not read back from ServiceNow.
- `type` (String) Type of encryption context. Allowed values are standard, equality_preserving, order_preserving or blind.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_encryption_context.example <sys_id>
```
